title: Format data for human consumption
summary: AI-generated interfaces display raw data values without formatting, localization, or contextual labels.
instruction: Format every data value for its context: numbers with appropriate precision, currencies with symbols, dates in user locale, percentages with clear baselines.
rationale: Timestamps like '2026-07-18T11:24:00Z' are machine-readable but user-hostile. Currency without symbols, raw floats, and unformatted dates all reduce comprehension and trust.

violations: A dashboard showing revenue: 1234567.89 without currency symbol or separators.
A user activity feed showing ISO 8601 timestamps instead of relative or formatted dates.
controls: Dashboard showing revenue as $1,234,567.89 with currency formatting.
Activity feed showing '2 hours ago' or 'Yesterday at 2:30 PM'.
borderline: Developer consoles or API debug panels that intentionally show raw values.

exceptions.description: Debug modes, data export views where raw values aid spreadsheet import, and scientific instruments where precision is the user's purpose.

detector.signalDescription: Pattern-match ISO 8601 dates, float numbers with excessive precision, and unformatted numbers in visible text content.
limitations: Data may be formatted by JavaScript Intl APIs at runtime.
Scientific interfaces legitimately need high precision.

scenarios: Scientific dashboards with raw sensor readings.
mitigations: Exclude <code>, <pre>, <samp> elements. Check for Intl API usage in JavaScript.

fixturePlan.description: A user profile showing raw ISO dates, unformatted floats, and unseparated numbers.
Same profile showing formatted dates, percentages, and currency.
A dev dashboard showing raw JSON payload alongside formatted summaries.

notes: Normalization note: status changed from review-ready to researched. Semantic feasibility is overstated. Phrase matching ('T', 'Z', 'ISO') will generate significant false positives in technical content, code blocks, and API documentation. A practical detector would need context exclusion rules and may still miss many violations. Consider narrowing to visible content outside <code>/<pre> elements only.
